hypothesis: If a closed-loop Bayesian optimization framework is applied to systematically explore the multidimensional parameter space of reactive carbon electrolyzers, then it will identify optimal combinations of bicarbonate concentration, temperature, flow rate, KOH concentration, and applied current density that achieve higher reactor yields more efficiently than manual experimentation, because Gaussian process regression can model complex nonlinear interactions between parameters and guide sequential experiment selection toward global optima with fewer total experiments.
